title: Exploratory factor analysis on numeric data
summary: Rust port of psych::fa: iterative principal-axis / minimum-residual factor extraction with Kaiser-normalized analytic Varimax rotation. Iterates over meta batches and a range of n_factors values, emitting one xlsx workbook with per-combination summary + loadings sheets.
description: For each batch and each n_factors in [min_count, min(max_count, n_vars-1)]: builds the correlation matrix, extracts factors (MinRes/PA both route to iterative principal-axis with SMC communality initialisation), rotates loadings, and writes eigenvalues (all dims: value, diff, prop, cumulative), per-variable loadings MR1..MRk, communality h², uniqueness u², Hoffman complexity (Σl²)²/Σl⁴, and dominant factor index. Rows are sorted within each sheet by dominant factor ascending then |loading| descending — matching psych's `fa.sort`. Sheet names follow `<batch>_k<n>_sum` and `<batch>_k<n>_load`, truncated to Excel's 31-char limit.
usage:
[["factor-analysis::fa"]]
input = "./data.sav"
run_dir = "./runs"
run_name = "fa1"
min_count = 2
max_count = 5
cor = "pearson"
fm = "minres"
rotate = "varimax"

# Inline meta: regex patterns + row entries. A column may land in multiple batches.
meta = [
  { match = "^att_", batch = "attitudes", type = "ordinal" },
  { match = "^sat_(.+)", batch = "satisfaction", label_template = "Satisfaction: ${1}" },
  { variable = "nps", batch = "outcomes", label = "Net Promoter Score" },
]

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: Path to the input file. Supports CSV (wide, one row per observation) or SPSS .sav/.zsav; extension chooses the reader. SPSS columns inherit variable labels and infer type from value labels (labelled → ordinal, else numeric).

arguments.name: meta
arguments.description: Either a path to a meta CSV (columns: variable, label?, batch?, type?), or an inline TOML list of entries. Inline entries are either a row (`variable = "..."`) or a regex pattern (`match = "..."`). All-matches-apply: a column may appear in multiple entries and thus multiple batches. Unmatched columns are silently dropped. Label templates support ${name}, ${0} (whole match), ${1}, ${2}, ... (captures).

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run; no '..', '/' or '\\'.

arguments.name: batches
arguments.description: Subset of batches to analyse. Empty = one FA per batch, or a single `all` batch if meta has no batch column.

arguments.name: min_count
arguments.description: Smallest n_factors to extract per batch.

arguments.name: max_count
arguments.description: Largest n_factors to extract per batch. Clamped to (n_vars - 1).

arguments.name: fm
arguments.description: Factoring method: minres|pa|uls|ols|wls|gls|ml|minchi|minrank|alpha. Currently minres and pa (iterative principal-axis); other values error at runtime pending Phase 3.

arguments.name: rotate
arguments.description: Rotation: none|varimax|quartimax|bentler_t|equamax|varimin|geomin_t|bifactor|promax|oblimin|simplimax|bentler_q|geomin_q|biquartimin|cluster. Currently none and varimax; other values error at runtime pending Phase 3.

arguments.name: correct
arguments.description: Continuity correction for tetrachoric/polychoric zero cells (matches psych defaults).

arguments.name: na_action
arguments.description: complete_cases (pairwise deletion) or fail (abort on NA).

arguments.name: headers
arguments.description: Whether CSV inputs have a header row.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Number of worker threads for parallel batch × n_factors fan-out and per-pair correlation parallelism. `1` (default) runs serially. Results are deterministic regardless of thread count.

arguments.name: output_format
arguments.description: Override stdout format: human, protocol, or quiet.

notes: Input formats: `.csv` / `.tsv` (CSV reader) or `.sav` / `.zsav` (SPSS via the bundled reader). SPSS user-defined missing values are coerced to NA on read.

Inline meta semantics: entries are evaluated in order; a column matching multiple entries is added multiple times (one per entry) and may participate in multiple batches. Set `type` to override the default (Numeric for CSV; SPSS-inferred for `.sav`). Set `label` / `label_template` to override the default label (column name for CSV; SPSS variable label for `.sav`).

Current delivery: full correlation family (Phase 2), MinRes/PA extraction, Varimax rotation. Remaining fm/rotate values are accepted by the validator but return a NotImplemented runtime error until Phase 3.
Sample size for each batch is the minimum non-zero n_obs across all correlation cells — a conservative choice matching psych::fa's default pairwise behaviour.
The `com` column is Hoffman's complexity: 1.0 means a variable loads on one factor; values approach n_factors as loadings are spread evenly.
